## Changes Made In This Block

- Updated `tools/build-native-artbook-apk.mjs` to accept:

## Install Boundary
Android rejects the patched local-debug APK as an in-place update because it is signed with this laptop's debug keystore, not the old computer's keystore. To install patched builds on the phone, use one of these paths:
- Preferred: copy `C:\Users\brown\.android\debug.keystore` from the old computer.
- Alternative: uninstall/reset the phone app once, then install this laptop's local-debug APK.

## Continuation Pass - Sales Desk Refunds
- Rechecked current adjacent-app expectations against official Shopify POS, Square Appointments and WhatsApp Business materials.
- Added the benchmark note that mature POS/service workflows need returns/refunds, staff permission gates, inventory/restock effects, customer records, reminders and accounting visibility.
- Replaced the one-tap Sales Desk refund with a proper `Refund desk`:
  - manager/staff permission gate stays enforced;
  - seller enters refund amount, reason and manager/proof note;
  - physical goods can be marked restock-eligible after condition check;
  - refund records are stored in `state.posRefunds`;
  - customer thread, email, business copy, follow-up, notification and provider/audit boundary are written;
  - accounting ledger now shows the original sale and refund as separate tap-open lines.
- Kept `App.posRefund(id)` as an alias so old buttons/tests still land on the better desk.
- Made Studio modal backdrops instant/opaque so sheets opened after account switches do not briefly ghost the previous screen.

## Continuation Pass - Route Canvas Support And Trust
- Rechecked the delivery/trust benchmark against Shopify local delivery and fraud-analysis docs. Delivery support needs order-specific fulfillment status, evidence review, payout/risk holds and clear party routing.
- Added exact `Order support` escalation from Route Canvas:
  - buyer, seller or courier can open a support/dispute case from a specific order;
  - support cases are stored in `state.supportIncidents`;
  - the order status and payout hold move to support review/trust hold;
  - the exact order event trail records the case;
  - buyer, seller and courier audiences receive a notification that opens the exact order;
  - related thread messages, emails and follow-ups are written;
  - fraud/damage/payout-risk reasons also create a Trust report tied to the order and support case.
- Added order-detail support case rendering so the issue stays visible beside proof, route events and payout status.
- Updated `tools/state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` with a new support/dispute trail check.

## Continuation Pass - Subscription Access And Cancellation
- Rechecked the subscription/media benchmark against current YouTube membership and Patreon cancellation expectations: paid member perks should be visible, cancellation should be confirmed and future billing stopped, access should remain through the paid period, and billing/support history should stay reachable.
- Added a joined-series `Subscription access` desk:
  - joined ateliers open to status, price, creator/platform fee split, renewal/access-until rule and policy text;
  - free and paid series show clear access, rights, renewal and cancellation/leave terms;
  - receipt history is shown directly in the desk;

  - creator thread, owner email, subscriber receipt email, follow-up and audience-scoped notification are written;
  - subscription purchase events now route back to the access desk instead of a generic item card.

- Updated `ARTBOOK_COMPETITOR_BENCHMARK_2026.md` with the subscription access implication for future passes.

## Continuation Pass - Courier Trip Sheet And Payout Ledger
- Rechecked the courier benchmark against current Uber Driver app and DoorDash Dasher expectations: riders need clear trip offers, going-online state, support/safety, detailed earnings, payout destination and cash/delivery issue handling.
- Added a rider-grade `Courier trip sheet`:
  - available jobs can open trip details before accepting;
  - accepted jobs show pickup/drop-off, score, ETA/window, proof checklist, payout amount and ledger status;
  - active trips expose proof, incident, cash reconciliation and request-payout controls in one place;
  - courier job cards now surface payout ledger status, not just a loose payout estimate.
- Added durable courier money/state trails:
  - `state.courierLedger` records accepted jobs, proof-pending state, ready/requested payout, cash review and M-Pesa-style payout context;
  - `state.courierIncidents` records route incidents with priority and exact order linkage;
  - cash reconciliation can record expected vs collected cash and hold payout on mismatch;
  - courier payout requests write order events, courier email, follow-up and exact order notifications.
- Strengthened incident handling:
  - courier incidents now create exact support cases, route events, payout holds, seller/buyer/courier notifications, seller email and dispatch follow-up;
  - safety, cash and damaged-package incidents become urgent and keep payout held for review.
- Updated `tools/state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` with a courier trip/payout/cash/incident flow.
- Updated `ARTBOOK_COMPETITOR_BENCHMARK_2026.md` with the courier continuation benchmark.

## Continuation Pass - Event Ticket Operations
- Re-read the screenshot handoff prompt and post-work checklist before the pass.
- Rechecked event/ticket expectations against current Eventbrite, Ticketmaster and Humanitix-style organizer/ticketing expectations: event tools need venue proof, attendee/ticket management, check-in/scanning, refunds/support, transfer/resale rules and traceable mobile ticket state.
- Upgraded tickets from a basic calendar pass into event operations:
  - `state.eventOps` stores venue proof, capacity, refund policy, door mode, resale cap and payout hold for each event;
  - `state.eventOpsEvents` records event submit, ticket sale, resale, support, transfer, check-in and door-ready actions;
  - ticket purchase now creates an entry code, ticket trail and exact Bell route;
  - ticket details now show entry pass status, door mode, resale cap, venue/refund rules, ticket trail and actions for door check-in, capped resale, transfer and support/refund;
  - event organizers now get an `Event operations` desk with readiness checks, attendee list, check-in count, operations trail and editable operations fields;
  - event creation now asks for date, capacity, resale cap, venue proof and refund/support policy;
  - resale is capped by event policy, ticket support creates a support case, transfer regenerates the entry code, and check-in writes organizer/buyer notifications, email and follow-up;
  - Bell notification routing now opens exact ticket and event operations records.
- Updated `tools/state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` with an event operations flow covering ticket issue, exact notification, capped resale, support incident, check-in, organizer event ops, door-ready state and custom event proof creation.
- Updated `ARTBOOK_COMPETITOR_BENCHMARK_2026.md` with the event ticket operations source spot-check and product implication.

## Backend Scaffold Pass - First Real API Slice
- User confirmed backend work is allowed to make the app real.
- Inspected the current extracted project and found the docs already referenced `server/src`, but no server scaffold existed in the current worktree.
- Added a plain Node.js local API scaffold:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` serves `http://127.0.0.1:8787` by default;
  - `server/src/storage.mjs` provides JSON dev storage, seed data and audit logging;
  - token auth supports register, login and `GET /api/me`;
  - profile/privacy updates, discover, feed/posts, messages, follow-ups, listings, delivery quote, trust Seals/reports, privacy export and deletion request endpoints are implemented;
  - provider-backed flows for payments, media upload URLs, calls and KYC fail closed with `provider_not_configured` unless explicitly configured for local demos;
  - `server/README.md` documents run commands and provider boundaries;
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` verifies the backend slice end to end.
- Updated `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` to note that the local scaffold now exists and what it covers.

## Backend Sync Pass - App-Facing API Connection
- Re-read the screenshot handoff prompt and post-work checklist before the pass.
- Compared the gap against offline-first/mobile sync expectations: local state should keep working without a network, sync should be explicit and auditable, and provider-backed money/media/KYC should fail closed until real credentials and webhooks exist.
- Added a working `Backend sync` desk inside the app:
  - persistent offline-first/local-API mode and API base URL;
  - `/api/health` and `/api/schema` connection test;

  - profile/status/privacy sync to `PATCH /api/profiles/me`;
  - one safe unsynced Stroke to `POST /api/posts`;
  - one safe follow-up to `POST /api/followups`;
  - one seller listing to `POST /api/listings` in review state;
  - payment provider probe that expects `provider_not_configured`;
  - local backend event trail and exact Bell route back to the sync desk.
- Added the backend connection card to Menu and Backend handoff.
- Corrected the handoff modal health endpoint text from `/health` to `/api/health` and added the physical-phone LAN-IP warning.
- Added `tools/backend-sync-ui-test.mjs`, which starts the real local server, drives the app UI through sync, then verifies both local app state and backend JSON store state.

  - readiness status was `fresh_install_possible`.

  - installed APK and target APK SHA-256 match;
  - installed APK and target APK certificate SHA-256 match;

  - added `.app.artbook-os` as the new shell flag;
  - replaced the phone shell with a calmer operating layer: compact top bar, teal status-strip contrast, single brand mark, tighter search/context pill and flatter bottom dock;

## Fundi Jobs Agreement And Provenance Pass
- Reworked Fundi Jobs so a bid is only a quote, not permission to begin work.
- Added a job agreement gate:
  - customer accepts a quote;
  - fundi must agree the price, scope, timing and proof checklist;
  - work, proof close-out and completion are blocked while either side has not agreed;
  - any later price/scope/date change becomes a change order that the other side must approve or reject before work continues.
- Added urgency and subtle client-signal logic:
  - customers choose urgency (`Normal`, `Soon`, `Urgent`, `Emergency`, `Privacy`);
  - urgency ranks visible work for fundis and adjusts demo quotes upward when faster work is requested;
  - client reliability stays as a quiet sort/quote signal rather than a loud public badge.
- Reworked Provenance Seals:
  - completed jobs count immediately and remain traceable whether or not a Seal is granted;
  - fundi service Seal is customer-controlled;
  - client reliability Seal is fundi-controlled and deliberately subtle;
  - Seal requests are blocked by a 24-hour cooling-off window after completion to reduce pressure/manipulation;
  - either side can request a Seal after the window, but only the receiving party can grant or decline.
- Added experience-weighted merit scoring:
  - score blends smoothed Seal rate with completed-job volume;
  - color/label bands now distinguish early proof from deep proof, so `1/1` cannot outrank a fundi with many jobs and strong Seal history.
- Verified against marketplace patterns:
  - Upwork fixed-price/milestone guidance: agree amounts, deliverables and deadlines before work starts; escrow and review before release.
  - Taskrabbit terms/payment guidance: task fee is agreed by client and tasker; payments stay on-platform.
- Verification after this pass:
  - `smoke-test-artbook.mjs`: passed.
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs`: 28 checks passed, including Fundi quote -> agreement -> proof -> customer-controlled Seal.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs`: 25 checks passed.
  - `visual-audit-artbook.mjs`: 88 screens checked, 0 problems.
  - `head-to-toe-audit-artbook.mjs`: 5 roles x 20 routes plus commerce/work/modal checks passed.
- Rebuilt `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\artbook-phone-install.apk`.
- APK SHA-256: `A4B3AEC2496ABEF8530882EABC0CD27E08A3D94FB9E9E0D73489AC2C4955B793`.
- Verified APK signatures: v1, v2 and v3 true.
- Installed updated APK on Motorola USB device `ZY22JSRL8G` with `adb install -r`; ADB returned `Success`.
- Android package verification after install: `versionCode=181`, `versionName=1.181`, `targetSdk=35`, `lastUpdateTime=2026-05-29 15:19:36`.

## Fundi Jobs Escrow Funding Gate
- Follow-up pass after the agreement/Seal work.
- Split the Fundi flow again so both-side agreement does not automatically mean escrow is funded:
  - customer accepts a quote;
  - customer and fundi both sign the job agreement;
  - job moves to `funding_pending`;
  - only the customer can fund escrow;
  - fundi cannot start while escrow is not funded;
  - proof submission and customer completion stay blocked unless agreement, funding and change-order gates are clean.
- Added a visible `pay` stage to the Fundi stage rail.
- Added `Fund escrow` actions and `Funding needed` status language on job cards and agreement panels.
- Updated tests to prove both blockers:
  - starting immediately after quote acceptance remains blocked at `terms_pending`;
  - starting after both signatures but before funding remains blocked at `funding_pending`.
- Verification after this pass:
  - `smoke-test-artbook.mjs`: passed.
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs`: 28 checks passed.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs`: 25 checks passed.
  - `visual-audit-artbook.mjs`: 88 screens checked, 0 problems.
  - `head-to-toe-audit-artbook.mjs`: 5 roles x 20 routes plus commerce/work/modal checks passed.
- Rebuilt `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\artbook-phone-install.apk`.

## Fundi Jobs Cancellation, Refund And Support Gate
- Follow-up pass after the escrow funding gate.
- Added a dedicated Fundi cancellation/refund desk:
  - before escrow funding, either job party can close the job cleanly with no money movement;
  - after escrow funding but before work/proof starts, the job closes and a full customer refund record is written;
  - after work or proof begins, cancellation opens a support review, escrow is held and no automatic refund is created;
  - completed jobs keep their completed state and stay traceable.
- Added exact trails for cancellation outcomes:
  - job event trail;
  - customer room message;
  - `fundiRefunds` ledger for funded pre-start refunds;
  - support incident with `trust hold` priority for post-start cancellation review;
  - Bell notification, email and follow-up records.
- Raised retained Bell notification history from 80 to 160 so dense exact workflow alerts do not evict important transaction routes during normal power-user sessions.
- Updated audits:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves funded pre-start cancellation writes a refund, while post-start cancellation goes to support with escrow held.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves pre-funding clean close, funded pre-start refund, and post-start support review as separate outcomes.
- Verification after this pass:
  - `smoke-test-artbook.mjs`: passed.

  - `head-to-toe-audit-artbook.mjs`: 5 roles x 20 routes plus commerce/work/modal checks passed.

## Refund Provider Operations Pass
- Follow-up pass after Fundi cancellation/refund work.
- Added a unified Refund Operations desk for provider-backed money states:
  - POS refunds and Fundi escrow refunds now appear in one provider queue;
  - rows are grouped into pending, needs review and succeeded states;
  - each row links back to its source receipt or Fundi job;
  - provider-pending refunds remain visibly unresolved until a provider result is recorded.
- Added provider result simulation for the prototype:
  - `provider succeeded` records a provider reference and keeps the refund linked to the ledger/customer trail;
  - `requires action` and `provider failed` create support/reconciliation work instead of pretending money moved;
  - Fundi provider failure moves escrow to `refund review` and creates a support incident.
- Added Fundi refunds to the accounting ledger so customers/fundis can see escrow refunds beside POS refunds, bookings, orders and boosts.
- Added Refund Ops entry point from Accounting Readout.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Stripe Refund object uses provider statuses including pending, requires_action, succeeded, failed and canceled.
  - Square Refund object exposes refund status including PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED and FAILED.
  - Shopify POS refunds foreground order-linked full/partial refunds, staff permissions, return reasons and restocking rules.

## Mobile Touch Target Accessibility Pass
- Follow-up pass after the Refund Provider Operations build.
- Addressed the remaining touch-size warnings from `accessibility-audit-artbook.mjs`:
  - widened and raised scrollable chips, market/register chips, booking tabs, podcast/world chips, profile chips and Find role tabs to a 48px minimum target on coarse/mobile screens;
  - preserved horizontal scroll behavior so the denser controls stay reachable without introducing page overflow;
  - kept the visual chip style intact while increasing the actual tappable region.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Android Accessibility guidance recommends touch targets at least 48dp by 48dp, with padding allowed to create the full target.
  - Android Developers accessibility guidance recommends each interactive UI element have a focusable/touch target of at least 48dp by 48dp.
  - WCAG 2.2 target-size minimum is 24 CSS px at Level AA, so Artbook is using the stricter Android mobile target for this APK.

## Semantic Media Accessibility Pass
- Follow-up pass after the 48px touch target cleanup.
- Cleared the remaining non-semantic clickable wrapper warnings from `accessibility-audit-artbook.mjs`:
  - added a shared `roleButton()` helper for custom clickable surfaces that need `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, an accessible label and Enter/Space activation;
  - applied it to feed/post headers, media preview frames, story bubbles, booking service matrix rows and guide/curator profile cells;
  - added a generic `[role="button"]:focus-visible` outline so the new keyboard targets are visible in every UI skin;
  - removed the non-action `onclick` wrapper from embedded sound ribbons and moved event isolation onto the real preview button;
  - raised the profile status edit button to a 48px mobile target.
- Current source spot-check:
  - WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Button Pattern says custom button widgets should expose the button role, have an accessible label and activate with Space or Enter.
  - WCAG 2.2 Keyboard guidance keeps functionality available from a keyboard interface.
  - Android Accessibility guidance still favors 48dp interactive targets on the APK surface, so the semantics pass kept the stricter mobile target rule.

## Fundi Escrow Top-Up Change Order Pass
- Follow-up pass after the semantic accessibility build.
- Closed a money-side gap in the Fundi marketplace:
  - funded jobs now preserve the actual held escrow amount separately from the revised agreement amount;
  - approved price increases after funding move escrow to `topup pending`, record `topupDue`, move the job back to `funding_pending`, and block work/proof until the customer funds the difference;
  - funding a top-up now records an `Escrow top-up funded` event and settles `fundedAmount` to the revised agreement amount;
  - approved price decreases keep work allowed but mark the surplus as refund/credit instead of silently pretending the exact revised amount was funded;
  - pre-start cancellation after a funded top-up change refunds only actual held escrow, not an unfunded revised price.
- Updated Fundi agreement UI:
  - agreement cards show funded amount, top-up due and surplus chips;
  - copy now explains that funded price increases require customer top-up and price drops keep surplus marked for refund/credit.
- Updated audits:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves a funded price increase blocks work until the customer funds the top-up.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves the same top-up gate writes notifications, room events and exact funded escrow state.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Upwork milestone editing guidance treats higher active milestone amounts as requiring extra escrow funding and lower amounts as leaving excess funds available.
  - Freelancer milestone payment guidance separates requested, funded, released, cancelled and disputed milestone states.
  - Fiverr-style order extras keep scope/price expansion explicit instead of silently changing the original order value.

## Fundi Escrow Surplus Credit Pass
- Follow-up pass after the top-up change-order gate.
- Closed the opposite money-side gap for funded price decreases:
  - when a funded change order lowers the agreed price, completion now releases only the revised agreement amount;
  - leftover escrow is recorded as a provider-pending Fundi surplus credit/refund;
  - surplus credits appear in Refund Operations and Accounting through the existing Fundi refund ledger;
  - provider success for a surplus credit settles only the surplus status and does not mark the whole completed job as refunded;
  - provider failure/action-needed creates a support review for the surplus credit while keeping the completed job traceable.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves funded price decreases create a provider-pending surplus credit, room event and follow-up.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves provider success settles the surplus credit without changing the completed job into a refunded job.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Upwork milestone editing guidance treats decreased active milestone amounts as leaving extra escrow funds available.
  - Freelancer milestone guidance separates funded/released/disputed states, so surplus/refund state should stay visible after completion.
  - Fiverr order extras keep added/changed scope explicit, which supports keeping lower-price revisions explicit too.

## Fundi Seal Cooldown Anti-Pressure Pass
- Follow-up pass after the escrow surplus-credit build.
- Tightened the Fundi Provenance Seal request workflow:
  - completed jobs without an existing Seal opening timestamp now backfill a 24-hour cooling-off window instead of becoming immediately requestable;
  - the Seal desk explains that each side gets one respectful request per completed job;
  - duplicate Seal requests for the same target are blocked even if stale request state exists;
  - early request attempts persist the backfilled window and do not create a request, notification or trust record;
  - customer final say for fundi service Seals and fundi final say for subtle client reliability Seals remain unchanged.
- Updated Fundi marketplace rules copy so the anti-pressure rule is visible beside quote, escrow, cancellation and change-order rules.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves a fundi cannot request a Seal during the 24-hour window and cannot send duplicate requests after the window opens.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves missing cooldown timestamps are backfilled to a future 24-hour gate and do not allow immediate requests.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Upwork's current end-of-contract feedback flow uses a post-contract feedback window, double-blind visibility and genuine-feedback rules.
  - Fiverr's current review flow opens after completion, uses review windows, delayed/private feedback behavior and support-only removal for policy violations.
  - Artbook keeps this lighter for a prototype: one post-cooldown request, recipient final say, and a completed job trail that still counts even without a Seal.

## Refund Provider Permission Boundary Pass
- Follow-up pass after the booking deposit refund provider queue.
- Tightened Refund Operations permissions:

## Support Case Detail And Exact Message Context Pass
- Follow-up pass from the consultation/work-message question, extended to support and dispute cases.
- Tightened support case routing:
  - added a universal support case work view for order, booking refund, Fundi refund, ride, ticket and POS refund incidents;
  - support Bell notifications now prefer `Open support case` when a support id is present;
  - support messages now include an `Open case` action plus source-record actions where available;
  - support `Message` opens the correct party conversation with `activeWorkChat=support:<case id>`;
  - new support-context messages inherit the support id and any order, booking, job, ticket or ride id;
  - support cases now accept notes, notify the affected parties, and can be marked resolved while preserving the source record trail;
  - ticket support notifications now carry the exact support id, not only the ticket id;
  - POS/refund support visibility now derives parties from the linked sale/refund record so customers and providers do not receive unopenable support notices.
- Updated tests:
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves order support opens an exact support case, routes support Message into the correct seller thread, saves support-tagged chat replies, adds exact case notes, resolves the case and updates the source order.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves ticket support notifications carry the support id and the support case opens the exact ticket route.

  - Stripe disputes foreground evidence, customer communication and clear dispute timelines for challenged payments.

## Evidence-Backed Provenance Seal Pass
- Follow-up pass on trust and Seal manipulation risk outside the Fundi-specific cooldown flow.
- Tightened the general `Give Provenance Seal` path:
  - loose Seals are now blocked when the current account has no completed proof record with the target account;
  - eligible evidence is derived from completed/delivered orders, completed bookings, accepted/released collaborations, checked-in events and delivery proof;
  - the Seal type is selected from the evidence record instead of a free-form user choice;
  - saved Seals now store `evidenceId`, `evidenceLabel` and source `record` metadata;
  - duplicate Seals from the same account to the same target for the same evidence record are blocked;
  - the empty state explains that a real completed interaction must happen before vouching.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves a loose evidence-free Seal is blocked and a delivered order can create an evidence-backed Seal with record metadata.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves evidence-free vouching is blocked, evidence-backed order Seals write proof metadata and duplicate evidence Seals are refused.
- Current source spot-check:
  - Upwork feedback is contract-bound, double-blind and public feedback appears only on paid contracts.
  - eBay feedback is tied to purchase history and displays verified-purchase context.
  - Airbnb service/experience reviews open after the service or experience ends.
  - Trustpilot foregrounds fake-review detection, flagged reviews and review integrity operations.
  - Artbook implication: Provenance Seals should behave like evidence-backed vouches, not popularity taps. A completed record must unlock the vouch, and duplicate or unsupported Seals should fail closed.
- Verification after this pass:

  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs`: 30 checks passed.
  - `smoke-test-artbook.mjs`: passed.

  - `visual-audit-artbook.mjs`: 88 screens checked, 0 problems.
  - `tap-audit-artbook.mjs`: 121 taps clicked, 0 failures.
  - `head-to-toe-audit-artbook.mjs`: 5 roles x 20 routes plus commerce/work/modal checks passed.

## Evidence-Aware Reports And Fundi Deadline Pass
- Follow-up pass from the report/scoring risk and the Fundi timing/extension request.
- Tightened trust reports:
  - report concerns now look for linked order, booking, Fundi job, support, ticket or collaboration evidence between the reporter and target;
  - unsupported reports are saved as `intake` and shown only as non-scoring intake to the reporter;
  - evidence-backed reports remain `open`, carry `evidenceId`, `evidenceLabel` and source `record` metadata, and can affect active trust review;
  - support-created trust reports now include support evidence metadata automatically.
- Added Fundi work-window logic:
  - job posting and agreement now include a work-window duration, defaulting to 4 days;
  - the deadline clock starts only after both sides agree, escrow is funded and the fundi starts work;
  - deadline status distinguishes on-track, fundi-late, customer-review-late, completed-on-time and completed-late states while still allowing the job to finish;
  - either side can request an extension after work starts, but the deadline changes only after the other side approves;
  - change orders can revise the work window, and started jobs recalculate the deadline from the original start time;
  - job detail, agreement desk, rules and customer room trail now expose the work window, deadline and extension decisions.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves on-time Fundi deadline creation, late-deadline display, mutual extension approval, and evidence-aware trust reports.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves funding alone does not start the clock, Fundi start does, self-approval of extensions is blocked, client approval moves the deadline, and intake reports do not affect active trust flags.

## Booking Protocol Party-Scope And No-Show Pass
- Follow-up pass on booking safety and role boundaries.
- Tightened booking protocol:
  - only the booking booker or provider can reschedule/cancel a booking, even when functions are called directly;
  - closed bookings cannot be rescheduled or cancelled again;
  - booker reschedules now respect the recorded included-reschedule limit and surface a provider-message path when the limit is reached;
  - successful reschedules increment a booking reschedule count and still update both calendars, messages, emails and notices;
  - only the provider can record a no-show;
  - provider-recorded no-shows write the booking thread, event trail, notice, email and follow-up;
  - no-shows update the client rule so future bookings require full payment until the provider removes the rule.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves an unrelated account cannot reschedule/cancel someone else's booking and the booker cannot exceed the included reschedule limit.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves outsiders cannot mutate booking protocol, provider no-shows close the booking, the client risk rule changes, and a later booking from that client requires full payment.

## Event Ticket Capacity And Role-Scope Pass
- Follow-up pass on event/ticket money, capacity, resale, support and door-check risk.
- Updated the durable prompt and 15-minute heartbeat automation to carry the new plugin/app list plus AI, backend, Play Store, realistic-content and in-app money-loop priorities.
- Tightened event/ticket operations:
  - ticket purchase now fails closed unless the event is verified/door-ready and below capacity;
  - ticket capacity counts non-cancelled tickets and blocks overselling;
  - ticket owners can list resale or transfer only before check-in/closure;
  - only ticket owners or organizers can open ticket support;
  - only event organizers can check in a ticket, save event operations or mark an event door-ready;
  - event operations remain visible to ticket holders as read-only while organizer controls stay hidden/guarded;
  - event operations trails now filter by audience for non-organizer views;
  - notification retention increased so exact record-routing survives richer demo activity.
- Updated tests:
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves outsiders cannot resell/support/check-in/mark-ready, owners cannot self check-in, organizers can check in, review-pending custom events block purchases, and a one-seat door-ready event cannot oversell.

## Account-Scoped Wallet Ledger And Request Settlement Pass
- Follow-up pass on AI/backend/money rail readiness and founder money-loop risk.
- Rechecked the handoff prompt, post-work checklist, Expo/Test Android guidance, Supabase backend guardrails and OpenAI Developers API-key boundary before changing the money flow.
- Current policy/source spot-check:
  - Google Play Payments policy keeps digital in-app content payment rules separate from physical goods and real-world services.
  - Google Play Financial Services policy treats wallet/money movement as a sensitive category that needs accurate disclosures, compliance and privacy handling.
  - Artbook implication: local wallet behavior can model the product, but real launch needs server-owned balances, provider webhooks, KYC/AML limits, fraud review, reconciliation and clear Play Store disclosures.
- Tightened wallet logic:
  - added `walletBalancesByAccount`, `walletLedger` and `walletRequests`;
  - internal sends now debit the sender and credit the recipient instead of touching a loose global balance;
  - money requests create pending request/ledger rows and do not move funds until the payer accepts;
  - request settlement now requires the requested payer, their finance unlock and account-specific money verification;
  - top-up/withdraw rows record M-Pesa-style provider fee previews and provider-pending state;
  - wallet UI now shows requests, ledger, account activity, fee-saved estimate, transfer limit and launch boundary;
  - backend sync now lists the wallet ledger as a server-owned launch requirement;
  - Artguide finance answers now explain the account-scoped ledger and backend/payment boundary.
- Updated tests:
  - `quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now proves internal send debit/credit, fee-saved ledger row, recipient thread notification, pending request state and payer-approved settlement.
  - `state-flow-audit-artbook.mjs` now proves the same flow in the full state-flow suite with exact wallet notification audience.

## Backend Wallet Replay And Sync Pass
- Follow-up pass on the wallet/backend seam after account-scoped wallet rows were added locally.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Identified the highest-risk mismatch: the app could create internal wallet sends, money requests, top-ups and withdrawals locally, but the local API scaffold had no safe wallet replay endpoint and Backend Sync could only probe payments.
- Added backend wallet replay support:
  - `server/src/storage.mjs` now seeds `walletBalances`, `walletLedger` and `walletRequests`.
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `GET /api/wallet/ledger` and `POST /api/wallet/ledger/replay`.
  - replayed wallet rows are stored as `client_replayed_not_settled` audit data, with provider-looking rows kept provider-pending instead of real settlement;
  - privacy/data-export categories now include wallet ledger and request rows;
  - schema now exposes a `finance` endpoint group.
- Wired the app Backend Sync desk:
  - `backendSynced` now tracks `walletLedger` and `walletRequests`;
  - Backend Sync now posts unsynced wallet ledger/request rows to `/api/wallet/ledger/replay`;
  - wallet replay creates a backend audit event and keeps the payment provider fail-closed probe;
  - follow-up sync now sends a small batch of pending follow-ups so identity-review reminders do not starve explicit user reminders.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` now documents the wallet replay endpoints and clarifies that replay is audit-only.
  - `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` now frames the next milestone as promoting replay into a true immutable server ledger.
  - `server/README.md` now warns that wallet replay is not a real wallet, provider settlement, KYC decision or source of spendable funds.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now proves schema finance endpoints, replay acceptance, readable wallet replay, wallet privacy export category and payment fail-closed behavior.
  - `tools/backend-sync-ui-test.mjs` now creates wallet send/request rows in the app, runs Backend Sync, proves synced wallet maps/events, and verifies persisted server rows remain `client_replayed_not_settled`.
- Verification after this pass:

## Wallet Replay Party-Scope Hardening Pass
- Follow-up pass on the backend wallet replay trust boundary.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Found and fixed a subtle replay authorization risk:
  - the wallet replay endpoint was adding the authenticated profile as a fallback party before checking whether the row belonged to that profile;
  - malformed or foreign wallet rows could therefore look related to the caller even when `from`, `to`, `account` and `parties` did not name the caller.
- Tightened backend replay:
  - replay rows are now accepted only when the authenticated profile is explicitly present in `from`, `to`, `account` or `parties`;
  - money requests also require explicit `from` and `to`;
  - rejected ledger/request counts are returned and audited;
  - stored replay rows no longer default `account` to the caller unless the row is explicitly single-party.
- Updated docs:
  - `server/README.md` now states the explicit-party rule.
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` now documents that wallet replay accepts rows only when the authenticated profile is explicitly present.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now attempts to replay a foreign wallet row and proves it is rejected, not visible, and not counted as accepted.
- Verification after this pass:
  - syntax check: passed for `server/src/server.mjs` and `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs`.
  - `backend-smoke-test.mjs`: 17 checks passed.
  - `backend-sync-ui-test.mjs`: 20 checks passed.
  - `server/src/server.mjs --check`: passed and still lists finance endpoints.
  - `smoke-test-artbook.mjs`: passed.
  - `phone-install-readiness.mjs`: APK still valid, but no ADB device connected.
- No APK rebuild was needed in this block because only the local API scaffold, backend tests and docs changed; the last built APK remains `B956996E94A1FE98A534919B2DEE371B6E9B8600D2B284A4554FD492753D716E`.
- Phone install status at `2026-05-29 21:30:17 +09:30`: not completed. `adb devices -l` still shows only `emulator-5562 offline`.

## Backend Auth Session Hardening Pass
- Follow-up pass on backend readiness now that profile sync, wallet replay and privacy export depend on bearer auth.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Found and fixed the highest-risk local API auth gap:
  - demo users were stored with plaintext `password: "demo"`;
  - login allowed an existing email through when the request omitted a password;
  - sessions had no expiry timestamp.
- Tightened the scaffold:
  - `server/src/storage.mjs` now hashes seeded and registered demo passwords with PBKDF2-SHA256;
  - legacy plaintext users in `server/data/dev-store.json` are migrated on `loadStore`;
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now requires a password for register/login, verifies via timing-safe hash comparison, deletes legacy plaintext password fields, and issues expiring bearer sessions;
  - the persistent `server/data/dev-store.json` was migrated: 2 users, 0 plaintext password fields, 2 PBKDF2 hashes.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` now states password hashing, required login password and session expiry.
  - `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` now clarifies that production still needs MFA/recovery/device-risk controls.
  - `server/README.md` now names the scaffold auth boundary.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now proves registration returns an expiring session, passwordless login fails closed, seeded users still log in after hash migration, users store hashes only, and sessions carry expiry timestamps.

## Backend Trust Evidence Boundary Pass
- Follow-up pass on backend Provenance/Trust now that wallet replay and auth are tighter.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Found a backend/UI mismatch:
  - the app blocks loose Provenance Seals and keeps evidence-free reports as intake;
  - the local API still accepted evidence-free Seals and counted every non-closed report against trust score.
- Tightened backend trust behavior:
  - `POST /api/trust/seals` now requires evidence metadata and returns `evidence_required` without it;
  - duplicate Seals from the same account to the same target for the same evidence are blocked with `duplicate_evidence_seal`;
  - `POST /api/trust/reports` now stores reports with linked evidence as `open`, and reports without evidence as non-scoring `intake`;
  - `GET /api/trust/:profileId` now returns `activeReports` and `intakeReports`, and trust score only subtracts active evidence-backed reports;
  - public trust reads now match the API contract and no longer require auth.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` now documents evidence-required Seals, duplicate blocking, active reports and intake reports.
  - `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` now says production still needs record verification, dispute outcome checks and moderation queues.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now proves evidence-free Seals are blocked, evidence-backed Seals score, duplicate evidence Seals are blocked, intake reports do not score, evidence-backed reports do score, and public trust reads work.

## Backend Order/Booking Evidence Verification Pass
- Follow-up pass on Provenance/Trust after evidence metadata became required.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Found the next trust-boundary gap:
  - the API required evidence metadata, but still trusted client-claimed `order:` or `booking:` identifiers;
  - the server had no minimal order/booking records for Seals or active trust reports to verify against.
- Tightened backend evidence:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `POST /api/orders/checkout`, `PATCH /api/orders/:id/status`, `POST /api/bookings` and `PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete`;
  - order/booking completion is party-scoped and requires proof metadata before the record becomes `verified_completion`;
  - Provenance Seals now require completed server-owned evidence, with both parties matching the authenticated profile and target;
  - trust reports without evidence remain non-scoring intake, while claimed but unverified evidence is rejected instead of becoming an active penalty;
  - privacy/export commerce counts now include party-scoped orders and bookings.
- Updated docs:

## Backend Customer-Final Evidence Confirmation Pass
- Follow-up pass on the order/booking evidence verification layer.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.

  - the product prompt says the customer/booker has final say on whether completed work can support a Seal.
- Tightened backend evidence confirmation:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now records per-party `completionProofs`;
  - seller delivery proof moves an order to `buyer_confirmation_required`, not trusted evidence;
  - provider completion proof moves a booking to `booker_confirmation_required`, not trusted evidence;
  - only buyer/booker-confirmed completion sets `verified_completion`, `completedAt` and evidence usable by Provenance Seals or active trust reports.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` and `server/README.md` now state the buyer/booker-final evidence rule.
- Updated tests:

## Backend Trust Report Moderation Pass
- Follow-up pass on the Provenance Seal/report scoring boundary.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass and kept the prompt focus on backend, Play Store readiness, realistic trust behavior and money/workflow safety.
- Found the next trust manipulation gap:
  - evidence-backed reports were verified, but the API needed a clearer scoring boundary for duplicate reports and Seal/report contradictions;
  - repeated or contradictory report activity should not instantly damage public trust before moderation.
- Hardened backend trust moderation:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now returns duplicate report metadata with `duplicate_evidence_report`;
  - repeated same-reporter reports for the same target/evidence are rejected instead of creating extra rows;
  - reports that contradict an existing Provenance Seal enter `conflict_review`;
  - linked/duplicate/conflict review rows are returned in `reviewReports` and stay non-scoring until a future admin moderation flow resolves them;
  - active trust score still counts unique open evidence-backed reports only.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now proves Seal/report contradictions go to non-scoring conflict review, duplicate report attempts are rejected with the original report id, and trust score/review counts stay stable.

## Backend Trust Moderator Resolution Pass
- Follow-up pass on the conflict-review queue.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before the pass.
- Found the next trust-readiness gap:
  - `conflict_review` reports were safely non-scoring, but there was no backend path for an independent moderator to resolve them;
  - a real Provenance Seal system needs the contradiction to become dismissible, upholdable, escalatable or evidence-pending without letting the reporter or target clear their own case.
- Added trust moderation scaffold:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `GET /api/moderation/trust-reports` and `PATCH /api/moderation/trust-reports/:id`;
  - local `admin`/`moderator` profile roles or `ARTBOOK_MODERATOR_PROFILE_IDS` can access the queue;
  - involved parties are blocked from resolving their own trust report;
  - decisions support `dismiss`, `uphold`, `request_more_evidence` and `escalate`;
  - dismissed reports close without score impact;
  - upheld conflict reports can revoke the contradictory Seal and make the report active-scoring;
  - public trust responses now expose `revokedSeals` while scoring only non-revoked Seals and unique active reports.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/backend-smoke-test.mjs` now verifies the moderation schema, moderator registration, non-moderator queue denial, conflict queue listing, party-resolution denial, moderator dismissal and stable post-dismissal trust score.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` and `server/README.md` now document the moderation queue, resolver decisions and scaffold RBAC caveat.

## Backend Trust Evidence Response Pass
- Follow-up pass on the Trust evidence follow-up work.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, and the latest progress log before the pass.
- Found the next backend-readiness gap:
  - the Trust desk could collect an involved party's proof response, but the proof still needed a server-owned endpoint and audit log path;
  - moderator-requested proof must stay non-scoring until a moderator reviews it, and non-parties must not be able to append evidence to someone else's report.
- Added backend evidence-response behavior:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `POST /api/trust/reports/:id/evidence-responses`;
  - only report parties can respond, closed reports are blocked, and responses are accepted only after a moderator requested more evidence;
  - optional evidence is verified against server-owned completed order/booking rows and must include both report parties;
  - accepted responses append `evidenceResponses`, set `moderationState` to `evidence_response_submitted`, set `followUpStatus` to `waiting_moderator_review`, keep scoring as `non_scoring_until_moderator_review`, and write `trust.report.evidence_response` audit rows.
- Wired the mobile bridge:
  - `src/artbook-mobile.html` now syncs Trust proof follow-ups to the backend response endpoint when a backend report id exists;
  - synced responses store backend response ids/evidence ids on the local follow-up, mirror returned backend evidence responses into the Trust row, and record a `Trust evidence response synced` backend event;
  - the Trust desk now labels submitted proof as `Evidence under review` while keeping the report non-scoring until moderator review.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` documents the new evidence-response endpoint;
  - `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` and `server/README.md` describe the party-response moderation flow.
- Updated tests:

## Backend Evidence Response Review Pass
- Follow-up pass on the backend Trust evidence response work.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, and the latest progress log before the pass.
- Found the next moderation gap:
  - server-owned evidence responses could be submitted and audited, but moderators still had only the whole-report resolver;
  - a submitted proof response needed its own accept/reject/request-another-proof action so the response trail stays reviewable without pretending it immediately changes public trust.
- Added response-specific backend review:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `PATCH /api/moderation/trust-reports/:id/evidence-responses/:responseId`;
  - only moderators can call it, involved parties are still blocked from self-moderating, closed reports are refused, and missing responses return `evidence_response_not_found`;
  - decisions are `accept`, `reject`, and `request_more_evidence`;
  - accepted proof is marked `accepted_by_moderator` and keeps the report `non_scoring_until_moderator_resolution`;
  - rejected proof is marked `rejected_by_moderator` and stays audit-visible without scoring;
  - another evidence request reopens the involved-party follow-up path while staying non-scoring;
  - every review writes `moderation.trust_report.evidence_response_review`.
- Updated moderator UI:
  - `src/artbook-mobile.html` now shows server-owned evidence responses in the backend report detail panel;
  - moderators can accept, reject or ask again from the evidence response row;
  - successful response review mirrors the backend report into the local Trust row, writes an `Evidence response reviewed` backend event, and notifies involved parties with `Backend evidence reviewed`;
  - profile-facing Trust outcomes now distinguish `Evidence accepted` and `Evidence rejected` from merely `Evidence under review`.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md` documents the new moderation endpoint;
  - `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md` and `server/README.md` describe response review after party proof submission.
- Updated tests:

  - `tools/backend-sync-ui-test.mjs` now verifies the moderator UI accepts a synced proof response, mirrors backend/local state, writes notice/event trails and persists the review audit.

## Settlement Audit Backend Pass
- Follow-up pass on money/backend readiness for Fundi and order money movement.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, and the latest progress log before the pass.
- Added a backend escrow settlement audit lane:
  - `server/src/storage.mjs` now seeds `settlementAudits`.
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `GET /api/settlements/escrow-audits` and `POST /api/settlements/escrow-audits`.
  - settlement audit replay accepts only rows where the authenticated profile is one of the row parties and the row has a linked record plus positive amount.
  - accepted rows are stored as `client_replayed_audit_only_not_settled`, `providerVerified=false`, and `spendable=false`.
  - foreign or malformed rows are rejected per row, and replay writes `settlement.escrow_audit.replay` audit events.
- Connected the mobile backend handoff:
  - `src/artbook-mobile.html` now finds Fundi escrow/release/refund states for the active account and sends them to the settlement audit endpoint during Backend sync.
  - the Backend sync desk now shows a `Settlement audit` row so escrow/refund state is visibly separate from wallet balance and provider settlement.
  - `backendSynced.settlementAudits` prevents duplicate local replay of the same escrow/refund state.
- Updated contracts and handoff notes:
  - `docs/API_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/BACKEND_HANDOFF.md`, and `server/README.md` now describe settlement audit replay as audit-only, provider-unverified and non-spendable.

## Settlement Exception Review Note Pass
- Follow-up pass on server-owned settlement exception review.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, and the latest progress log before the pass.
- Added a moderator/support-only settlement review-note endpoint:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `POST /api/settlements/exceptions/:id/review-notes`.
  - allowed decisions are `hold_payout`, `hold_refund`, `await_provider`, `send_to_support`, and `mark_duplicate_note`.
  - non-moderator parties receive `403 forbidden`.
  - review notes append to the settlement audit row with `nonSettling:true`, `reviewState:"operator_note_recorded"`, reviewer id/role and next action.
  - the helper snapshots and restores `settlementStatus`, `providerStatus`, `providerVerified`, `spendable`, `amount`, and `state`, so a review note cannot settle payout, complete refunds, mark provider success, or create spendable balance.

## Settlement Receipt Candidate Intake Pass
- Follow-up pass on provider receipt/reconciliation scaffolding.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, and the latest progress log before the pass.
- Added audit-only receipt candidate intake:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `POST /api/settlements/exceptions/:id/receipt-candidates`.
  - the route is moderator/support-only, requires provider receipt id and idempotency key, and records signature status as `missing`, `unverified`, `invalid`, or `verified_placeholder`.
  - duplicate provider/idempotency keys return the original candidate and do not append a second provider receipt candidate.
  - receipt candidates update `providerReceipt`, `latestProviderReceiptCandidate`, `providerReceiptCandidates`, `supportStatus`, and the support timeline as `nonSettling:true`.
  - the route snapshots and restores `settlementStatus`, `providerStatus`, `providerVerified`, `spendable`, `amount`, and `state`, so receipt intake cannot mark provider success, release payout, complete refunds, or create spendable balance.

  - fixture templates cover M-Pesa/Daraja STK callbacks, card checkout settlement callbacks and payout/disbursement callbacks.
  - each template includes a realistic sample payload, raw-body signature-verification notes, replay-protection notes, settlement gates, and an embedded dry-run preview.
  - templates can bind to a settlement exception via `exceptionId`, `auditId` or `sourceId`.
  - templates remain `fixture_templates_only_no_settlement`; they do not audit, store receipt candidates, mark provider success, release payout/refund or create spendable balance.
  - the webhook parser now maps common card/payout identifiers such as `chargeId`, `paymentIntentId`, `payoutId` and `transferId` into receipt-candidate dry-run fields.
- Updated the phone Backend sync dry-run:

## Settlement Webhook Event Replay Ledger Pass
- Follow-up pass on provider webhook replay safety and backend readiness.

  - `POST /api/settlements/webhooks/:provider` still returns `503 provider_not_configured`, but now records replay-only event metadata with payload digest, payload shape, provider event id, provider receipt id, raw status, signature status/source, idempotency decision, duplicate link, target link, mismatch reasons and non-settling guard fields.

## Settlement Webhook Event Decision Pass
- Follow-up pass on Review Ops classification for provider callback replay evidence.

- Added a non-settling webhook-event decision scaffold:
  - `server/src/server.mjs` now exposes `POST /api/settlements/webhook-events/:id/review-decisions` for Review Ops/support/admin accounts.
  - allowed decisions are `duplicate`, `signature_invalid`, `needs_provider_fetch` and `ready_for_receipt_candidate`.
  - decisions are stored on the replay-ledger event as review metadata with `webhook_event_decision_only_no_settlement`, `providerVerified:false`, `spendable:false` and `provider_not_configured`.
  - `ready_for_receipt_candidate` returns a suggestion-only receipt payload; it does not create a provider receipt candidate, mark provider success, release payout/refund or create spendable balance.

## Release Evidence Packet Pass

## Safety Pass: Adult Vaults And Fundi Job Boundaries
- Follow-up pass on whole-app people safety after the user clarified that fundis and customers should see real names, but job relationships must not expose creator/fan/private content.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, Android emulator QA guidance, and Codex Security scan guidance. A formal repository-wide Codex Security scan still needs explicit subagent authorization, so this pass stayed as a focused product-safety/code pass.
- Added 18+ subscription vault safeguards:
  - `src/artbook-mobile.html` now includes a seeded non-explicit `After Dark verified vault` demo marked 18+.
  - added `adult` identity scope with 18+ age-check readiness, explicit-content policy copy and role registration.
  - adult vault access now requires adult verification plus an explicit warning/consent receipt before subscribing.
  - protected adult viewer uses non-explicit placeholder media, per-viewer watermarking, access/view audit rows, leak/coercion report queue and download/screen-record safety copy.
  - native Android build bridge now exposes `setSecureMode(...)` and the web app requests secure-content mode for protected viewer modals.
- Added Fundi/job safety boundaries:
  - job cards/detail/rooms keep real customer and fundi names visible for accountability.
  - job relationships now show compact `real names` and `content isolated` tags.
  - a new Fundi safety desk explains that a job does not unlock adult vaults, subscriber media, social posts, private galleries, direct contact, calls or full profile content.
  - exact site/private address handoff needs both sides to consent and still stays gated until agreement/escrow funding.
  - seeded and newly posted Fundi jobs now carry `real_names_content_isolated` privacy metadata.
- Adjacent safety scan:
  - inspected profile/contact/address/media surfaces with `rg`; delivery and ride flows already use masked contacts/address privacy, while Fundi jobs and subscriber/adult media were the main gaps fixed in this pass.
  - docs now call out adult-content compliance, non-explicit demo boundary, backend-enforced watermarking/moderation, and real-name job content isolation.
- Updated tests:
  - `tools/quality-loop-artbook.mjs` now verifies adult vault gate/verification/consent/watermark/secure-mode/leak-report behavior.
  - `tools/quality-loop-artbook.mjs` also verifies privacy-sensitive Fundi jobs show real names while keeping private/adult/subscriber content isolated and site handoff gated before funding.

## AI Prompt Shield And Model Gateway Preview Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 10:49:00 +09:30` continuing the month-long Artbook end-to-end pass. No explicit OpenAI API key decision was provided, so this pass stayed provider-agnostic and did not add live OpenAI calls.

  - added prompt-injection detection for instruction override, prompt exfiltration, role impersonation, tool forcing and policy-bypass phrases in visible request/message/post/follow-up/trust/support text.
  - `safeAiText()` now redacts instruction-like phrases as data markers such as `[instruction-like text treated as data]` and `[exfiltration request treated as data]`.
  - tightened secret redaction so `token=...` hides the token without swallowing following text that still needs prompt-shield handling.
  - AI context previews now include `promptInjectionDefense` metadata and a high-risk `prompt_injection_attempt` flag when needed.
  - added a `modelGateway` preview that reports provider credential status, outbound policy, output policy and `model_gateway_preview_only_no_external_call`; live calls remain disabled and no protected action can be returned.

## Masked Calls And Artbook Relay Privacy Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 13:06 +09:30` after the user asked for Uber-style calling where businesses, clients, riders, couriers and other allowed parties can call without seeing each other's real phone numbers.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added a single call policy for chats, orders, bookings, freelancer jobs, receipts/invoices, pickup prompts and support cases.
  - kept ordinary messages open, but made phone-line fallback context-bound: public profiles, fan-to-creator access, inactive records and business chats without a booking/order/support record stay message-first.
  - added `maskedCallSessions` with `realNumbersExposed:false`, caller/callee number exposure flags, provider status, context key, expiry copy and `Artbook Relay XX-###` aliases.
  - changed call options so app-to-app audio/video comes first; the last-resort real-line path is a masked relay request only.
  - made masked phone fallback fail closed in the prototype with `telephony_provider_not_configured`; the UI records the audit/session but never dials a real line or opens `tel:`.
  - updated work-boundary copy so exact address, route notes and phone fallback stay inside the exact work room and phone-line calls must route through Artbook Relay.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\server.mjs`:
  - expanded `POST /api/calls` from a generic provider failure into a masked-relay fail-closed contract.
  - response now returns `callPrivacy.realNumbersExposed:false`, `maskedRelayRequired:true`, allowed call contexts, requested context, provider boundary and next action.
  - audit rows now persist `provider.fail_closed` for `calls/masked_relay` without storing or echoing raw party phone numbers.

## Masked Call Backend Validation Hardening
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 13:14:24 +09:30` continuing the provider-hardening pass for Uber-style private calling.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\server.mjs`:
  - added server validation for masked-call requests before provider handoff.
  - requires a server-owned active context: order/delivery/ride via server order records, booking via server booking records, or support via support incidents.
  - proves the caller and requested peer are parties on that record; foreign users now receive `call_context_forbidden` before any provider handoff.
  - blocks missing context as `call_context_required`, inactive records as `call_context_inactive`, invalid peers as `call_peer_invalid`, and unsupported/missing records before provider handoff.
  - adds a 5-attempt-per-hour context rate-limit scaffold, expiry windows, app-call-first policy, consent/recording policy flag and provider-called false metadata.
  - stores blocked attempts as `calls.masked_relay.blocked` and valid provider attempts as `provider.fail_closed` with no raw caller/callee number echo.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs`:
  - verifies missing-context relay requests are blocked without raw-number leakage.
  - verifies non-party relay requests are forbidden without provider handoff.
  - verifies valid order-context relay requests include context validation, party verification, expiry, rate-limit and consent policy while still failing closed as `provider_not_configured`.
  - verifies blocked and provider-fail-closed audit rows persist the number-privacy boundary.
- Updated docs:
  - `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\API_CONTRACT.md`
  - `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\README.md`
- Verification after this pass:

## Delivery Dispatch Backend Scaffold
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 14:49 +09:30` for the next app-owned high-risk area after the external OpenAI quota blocker.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`; the next highest-risk gap was delivery/transport because `docs\API_CONTRACT.md` described dispatch endpoints but the server only exposed delivery quotes.

  - added schema entries for delivery dispatch routes.
  - added `POST /api/couriers/register` as review-only courier onboarding with KYC/payout still pending.
  - added `POST /api/delivery/jobs` to create server-owned dispatch rows tied to orders, with address tokens, masked-contact policy, proof requirements and seller/courier payout holds.
  - added `GET /api/delivery/jobs/available` for courier-only ranked job offers without raw phones, exact addresses or contact tokens.
  - added `POST /api/delivery/jobs/:id/accept` so a courier can claim a job with masked contacts active and payout still held.
  - added `PATCH /api/delivery/jobs/:id/status`, `POST /api/delivery/jobs/:id/proof` and `POST /api/delivery/jobs/:id/incidents` with proof/PIN hashing, incident support rows and no money movement.
  - connected delivery jobs into masked-call active-context lookup and AI context previews as visible delivery records with a delivery risk flag.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\storage.mjs` with empty delivery/courier seed arrays.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs` to exercise courier onboarding, dispatch job creation, courier availability ranking, accept, proof hashing, incident support creation, AI delivery risk counting and no raw phone/address leakage.

## Courier Shift, Payout Review And Delivery Webhook Scaffold
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 15:31 +09:30` for the next highest-risk app-owned backend gap after the Moto World sheet fix.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`; the next gap was delivery/transport provider readiness because the API contract promised courier shift state, courier payouts and delivery provider webhooks while the server only handled onboarding, dispatch jobs, proof and incidents.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\server.mjs`:
  - added `deliveryProviderEvents` to the delivery store guard.
  - added schema entries for `PATCH /api/couriers/me/shift`, `GET /api/couriers/me/payouts` and `POST /api/delivery/webhooks/:provider`.
  - added courier online/offline/paused shift state with review-only dispatch eligibility, low-bandwidth/cash flags and device-location evidence that stores a digest instead of exact coordinates.
  - added courier payout review that lists assigned delivery jobs, held payout estimates, required KYC/provider evidence and zero settled/spendable money.
  - added moderator-only delivery provider webhook replay that stores payload digest/shape/signature status, attaches unverified provider events to target jobs when found, and fails closed without dispatch or payout changes.
  - added courier payout amount capture on delivery jobs for review-only payout evidence.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\storage.mjs` with the new `deliveryProviderEvents` seed array.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs` to cover shift privacy, payout review, non-moderator webhook blocking, moderator webhook replay and no raw phone/address/coordinate leakage.
- Updated docs:
  - `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\API_CONTRACT.md`
  - `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\BACKEND_HANDOFF.md`
  - `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\PLAYSTORE_PROTOTYPE_NOTES.md`

## Low-Stock Reorder And Supplier Proof
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 18:34 +09:30` after the stock movement ledger pass, focusing on low-stock reorder points, supplier purchase orders, receiving proof and accounting visibility.
- Benchmarked against official current docs:
  - Shopify purchase orders, suppliers and receiving inventory from POs.
  - Square low-stock inventory alerts.
  - Lightspeed X-Series reorder point recommendations and purchase orders.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added persistent `stockReorderRules` and `stockPurchaseOrders` state.
  - added reorder point, restock target, supplier, supplier ref/SKU, lead time, payment terms, order quantity and proof note controls to item Stock/capacity.
  - added a Reorder queue to AI stock intake so low-stock items show supplier/order suggestions instead of only counts.
  - added supplier PO lifecycle: `draft` -> `ordered` -> `received`.
  - draft and ordered POs do not mutate inventory; receiving a PO increases stock and writes `Supplier purchase order received` to the stock movement history.
  - separated order confirmation from receiving proof so the final audit note keeps the supplier invoice/delivery/photo requirement instead of overwriting it.
  - supplier POs now appear in Accounting readout, accounting ledger and accounting export as `Supplier PO` / `Stock purchase order` rows.
  - business low-stock alerts now use each item's reorder point instead of a hard-coded `<= 2` count.

## Restricted Media Safety Backend Queue
- Follow-up at `2026-05-30 20:43 +09:30` after re-reading the prompt memory/checklist and selecting the next high-risk gap: adult leak/coercion reports were local-only support rows while trust moderation already had a server-owned queue.

  - added `restrictedMediaReports` to the JSON dev store.
  - added `POST /api/restricted-media/reports` for metadata-only restricted-media safety reports.
  - raw media payload fields such as `rawMedia`, `video`, `image`, `file`, `base64` and `dataUrl` are rejected with `raw_media_not_accepted`.
  - each report stores viewer/reporter, creator owner, vault/content id, watermark/evidence reference, urgent support incident, non-scoring safety state and `rawMediaStored:false`.
  - added moderator-only `GET /api/moderation/restricted-media-reports` and `PATCH /api/moderation/restricted-media-reports/:id`.
  - moderator decisions can request more evidence, recommend a temporary visibility hold, dismiss or escalate, but provider media action remains `not_called_provider_fail_closed`.

  - adult leak/coercion reports now become metadata-only restricted-media safety rows with `contentAction:"review_hold_recommended"` and `rawMediaStored:false`.
  - backend sync can hand those reports to the new server endpoint when connected.
  - backend sync status now includes a `Restricted media safety` row so Review Ops can see whether this lane is server-ready.

  - `tools\backend-sync-ui-test.mjs` now expects the restricted-media safety backend row.

## Sound Ribbon Fast Lane Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 05:55:07 +09:30` on the active month-long Artbook product and QA goal.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md` and `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md` before editing.

- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:

## Keyboard-Aware Compose Sheet Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 06:28:06 +09:30` on the active month-long Artbook product and QA goal.

  - changed the modal backdrop from fixed `inset:0` to explicit visual-viewport-aware bounds when the keyboard is open.
  - limited sheet transitions to `transform` only so `max-height` no longer animates slowly through the keyboard clamp.

## Founder-Led Backend AI Contract Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 11:14:39 +09:30` from the 10-minute founder-led loop.
- Product direction handled this cycle:
  - tightened the live-AI/backend truth surface so the app is clearer for founder QA and safer for launch prep.
  - Backend sync now states that live AI must be server-owned: the raw OpenAI key never belongs in the APK, quota/billing and rate limits belong behind the backend, and Android can still provide local/offline guidance while provider-backed AI is unavailable.
  - Protected actions are now explicit in the AI contract: AI cannot move money, release escrow, approve identity/KYC/KYB, mint Provenance Seals, moderate restricted media, settle payouts or publish final compliance decisions.
  - Artguide and workflow search now route phrases such as live AI, OpenAI key, quota/billing, model gateway and server AI to the Backend AI contract.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added `AI launch gates` inside Backend sync.

## Motorola Command Latency Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 13:26:00 +09:30` after owner reported that taps/commands across the app felt too slow on the Motorola.
- Product/performance direction handled this cycle:
  - treated the issue as app-wide phone command latency, not a broken install.
  - kept Android in `phone` speed mode with a lighter Business Home, then expanded the same idea to heavy routes.
  - limited phone rendering on large list pages so Discover, Live, Audio, Tour, Worlds and Inbox show the useful first slice with search/filter paths to the rest instead of drawing dozens of cards at once.
  - replaced the full hidden Menu payload on Android with a fast command menu that still exposes business actions, main rooms, workflow search, phone fit, AI controls, privacy, backend, trust and Moto World.
  - queued navigation-only saves in the background on Android so route taps do not block on a full localStorage write.
  - fixed the shared hot path where `visiblePeople()` recalculated Moto World state for every synthetic account; it now checks Moto World enabled state once per route filter.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added phone route helpers: `routeLimit()`, `routeSlice()`, `phoneMoreRow()` and `queueNavigationSave()`.
  - added Android phone-speed list caps for Discover, Live, Audio, Tour, Worlds and Inbox.
  - added a lightweight `renderPhoneMore()` path for Android Menu.
  - added compact Android Studio rendering.
  - skipped unnecessary audio sync and dock color sampling on phone routes unless audio is active.
  - throttled Moto World automatic render ticks and stopped Moto World state normalization from running inside every visible-person filter.
- Motorola route benchmark after install:
  - Home `99 ms`, Menu `96 ms`, Discover `124 ms`, Live `62 ms`, Audio `66 ms`, Tour `114 ms`, Worlds `113 ms`, Studio `61 ms`, Inbox `112 ms`, Jobs `73 ms`, Calendar `61 ms`, Market `152 ms`.
  - Earlier measured problem areas were Home about `14,075 ms`, Menu about `1,295 ms`, Discover/Live/Audio/Tour/Worlds/Studio about `700-800 ms`.

  - phone install readiness: `ready_in_place_signature_matches`; installed APK hash matches target APK hash.

## Package Billing + Business Staff AI Mentor Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 14:01:50 +09:30` from the owner request to fix package delivery from the Delivery sheet and strengthen the business account/staff/AI workflow.
