## Live AI Recovery Phone Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 15:53:40 +09:30` after the owner confirmed the existing OpenAI key should be reused and credits are now available.
- Signal reviewed:
  - confirmed `.env.local` contains a redacted `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ARTBOOK_AI_LIVE=1`, and `OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4.1-mini`.
  - confirmed the backend is already running at `http://127.0.0.1:8787`.
  - confirmed the previous issue was not the key path anymore; the phone could not reach the laptop's LAN address because the local server is bound to `127.0.0.1`.
- Setup performed:
  - kept the OpenAI key server-side only; no key was placed in the APK or printed.
  - added an ADB reverse tunnel for the plugged-in Motorola: `adb -s ZY22JSRL8G reverse tcp:8787 tcp:8787`.
  - set the Motorola app's Backend Sync mode to `Local API server` with base URL `http://127.0.0.1:8787`, which now reaches the laptop backend through USB.
- Verification:
  - laptop backend health check passed at `http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/health`.
  - direct backend `POST /api/ai/live-assist` as `riley_biz` returned:
    - `status=ai_live_assist_server_side_guarded_no_sensitive_actions`
    - `providerStatus=live_server_side`
    - `liveCallsEnabled=true`
    - `gatewayStatus=model_gateway_live_server_side_guarded`
    - `model=gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14`
    - no `providerActionRequired` and no `providerError`.
  - phone shell `curl http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/health` succeeded through ADB reverse.
  - WebView/CDP Motorola check opened Backend Sync, connected successfully, then asked Artguide from the app:
    - Artguide card showed `Artguide live AI answered`.
    - card showed `live server-side` and `gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14`.
    - quota/failed banners were absent.
    - protected actions remained blocked: money movement and sensitive actions stayed off.
    - document overflow stayed `0`.
- Moto World:
  - no Moto World item was archived because this was a live AI infrastructure/configuration recovery pass.
- Source/APK:
  - no source file change and no APK rebuild were needed for this pass; the fix was backend credentials/credit availability plus Motorola USB routing and saved app backend settings.
- Blockers / notes:
  - live AI on the Motorola currently depends on the laptop backend staying running and the ADB reverse tunnel staying active while the phone is connected by USB.
  - if the phone is unplugged, ADB restarts, or the laptop server stops, Artguide will fall back again until the tunnel/server is restored or the backend is run on a LAN-accessible host.
  - production must use a hosted backend or a properly secured LAN/dev server; the Android APK must still never contain the OpenAI API key.
- Next focus:
  - continue business setup/package delivery simplification, and consider adding a one-tap developer health card that distinguishes `key/quota`, `server running`, `phone tunnel`, and `backend mode` so live AI failures are easier to understand on the phone.

## Device Fit And Auto-Rotate Phone Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 16:08:50 +09:30` after the owner reported pages were not aligning to phone size and the app did not change on auto-rotate.
- Signal reviewed:
  - the app already had a JavaScript fit sensor, but too many global shells and overlays still used raw `100vw` / `100dvh` sizing.
  - the native Android wrapper was still hard-locking `MainActivity` with `android:screenOrientation="portrait"`, so the WebView could not receive real orientation changes.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - made the measured `--fit-vw`, `--fit-vh`, `--fit-layout-height`, and `--fit-shell` values govern the app shell.
  - added a device-fit governor for the main surface, sheets, chat pages, letter chat, dock, mini-player, modal keyboard state, very narrow phones, and overflow repair.
  - added `scheduleDeviceFitRepair()` so post-render overflow is detected and narrow-grid fallbacks can self-correct.
  - kept landscape/tablet fit centered while compact phones fill the real phone width.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\build-native-artbook-apk.mjs`:
  - changed Android orientation from portrait-locked to `android:screenOrientation="fullUser"` so Artbook honors the phone's auto-rotate setting.
  - expanded `configChanges` to include `smallestScreenSize` and `screenLayout` so the WebView receives orientation/screen-size changes cleanly.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\quality-loop-artbook.mjs`:
  - added a static regression that fails if the Android wrapper returns to portrait lock.
  - expanded automatic fit checks across `360x720`, `320x640`, and `820x620`, including app shell, dock, sheet bounds, density marker, and horizontal overflow.
- Verification:
  - bundled Node `tools\quality-loop-artbook.mjs`: passed, 82 checks, 0 failures, 0 page errors, 0 console errors.
  - rebuilt signed APK with `tools\build-native-artbook-apk.mjs`; v1/v2/v3 signing verified.
  - installed on Motorola with `adb -s ZY22JSRL8G install -r artbook-phone-install.apk`: success.
  - launched on Motorola with `adb shell monkey`: success.
  - Motorola WebView/CDP portrait check after wake/unlock:
    - `innerWidth=433`, `innerHeight=964`, `screenOrientation=portrait-primary`, `data-fit=compact`, `data-orientation=portrait`, overflow `0`.
    - app shell fit inside `0..434`, dock fit inside `0..434`.
  - Motorola WebView/CDP forced landscape check:
    - `innerWidth=964`, `innerHeight=433`, `screenOrientation=landscape-primary`, `data-fit=tablet`, `data-orientation=landscape`, overflow `0`.
    - app shell centered at width `760`, dock centered at width `540`.
  - restored Android rotation mode to `free`; `accelerometer_rotation=1`.
  - ADB reverse for live AI remained present: `tcp:8787 -> tcp:8787`.
- Rebuilt APK SHA-256: `E761BF5D36B72A6888D1C4DF4D5072ACC929FD4884DD3E8E1A537687679321AB`.
- Moto World:
  - no Moto World item was archived because this was a direct owner-reported device-fit/orientation bug.
- Blockers / notes:
  - `git` remains unavailable on this Windows PATH.
  - APK remains debug-signed and prototype-only until release signing and provider/backend readiness are completed.

## Live AI Floating Chat and Operate Mode Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-05-31 18:47:34 +09:30` from the owner request that Live AI should behave like a continuous chat box, float while the app is being used, and operate the app safely.

  - added contextual follow-up routing so short replies like `yes`, `open it`, `show me`, `continue`, `what next`, and `explain that` keep the previous Live AI route instead of starting from scratch.

## Play Billing Subscription Handoff Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-06-01 05:58 +09:30` from the founder/product heartbeat, tightening paid digital subscription launch readiness before Android Play Store release.
- Current-policy references checked:
  - Google Play Payments policy says Play-distributed apps that require or accept payment for in-app features, app functionality, digital content or subscriptions must use Google Play's billing system unless a listed exception applies, and it separately lists physical goods/services and peer-to-peer payments as cases where Play Billing must not be used.
  - Android Play Billing docs say the app integrates through the Play Billing Library and should use a secure backend for purchase verification, subscription-specific flows and Real-time Developer Notifications.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - tightened the Play Billing subscription handoff so paid vaults include numeric price and paid-access flags.

  - added copyable billing packet language for product catalog mapping, Android Billing Library purchase flow, server purchase-token verification, entitlement restore/cancel/refund handling, partner payment boundary and founder revenue reporting.

## Pay Lens Backend Validation Contract Pass

## Pay Lens Extraction Handoff Contract Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-06-01 12:54 +09:30` from the founder/product heartbeat, moving Pay Lens invoice/screenshot/QR extraction toward a backend-owned handoff boundary.
- Product research note:
  - checked Wise QR/Quick Pay behavior at a high level: useful ingredients are scan/import document or photo, review the amount/details, then confirm; Artbook keeps its own Pay Lens naming/copy and does not copy Wise branding or assets.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\server\src\server.mjs`:
  - added authenticated `POST /api/pay-lens/extract-draft`.
  - accepts invoice, screenshot/photo, QR image metadata or QR text plus redacted OCR summaries.
  - rejects raw file bytes, base64/data URLs and unsupported file types before parsing.
  - extracts or prepares placeholder review fields for payee, amount, currency, masked payment details, reference and due date, then runs the existing dry-run draft validator.
  - audits only file metadata, digests, detected rail and missing-field state.
  - stays `pay_lens_extraction_handoff_only_no_settlement` with raw file storage, provider OCR/QR calls, wallet credit, escrow release, payout and founder revenue recognition all disabled.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs`:
  - schema now verifies the extraction endpoint.
  - smoke tests reject raw/base64 files and unsupported file types.
  - smoke tests prepare invoice and QR text drafts, prove M-Pesa PayBill/Till classification, verify no money movement, and confirm raw invoice/payment details are not returned or stored in audit logs.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\API_CONTRACT.md` and `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\docs\BACKEND_HANDOFF.md`:
  - documented the extraction handoff route and no-raw-file/no-settlement boundaries.

  - bundled Node `node --check tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs`: passed.

- Moto World:
  - no Moto World item was archived because this was direct founder/product Pay Lens backend hardening, not a Moto World-supplied issue.
- Blockers / notes:
  - production still needs provider-owned object storage upload URLs, real OCR/QR parser workers, provider rail validation, KYC/limits/fraud checks, signed webhooks and reconciliation before any payment can move.

## Dribbble-Inspired Interface Edition Refresh
- Follow-up at `2026-06-01 16:35 +09:30` after the founder asked to study Dribbble and redo the UI so themes feel like distinct product experiences, not different colouring.

## Package Route Review Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-06-02 01:54 +09:30` from the automated founder/product QA loop after the Appointment Command pass pointed at package quote/review screens.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, latest QA progress, and Android QA guidance before editing.
- Highest-value launch-readiness move:
  - inserted a required package route review step between the sender form and route creation/client billing.
  - kept delivery Play Store-safe and provider-led: the app now stores a temporary package draft, shows the fee/proof/billing boundary, and only creates the local route/client bill after the owner taps confirm; no live dispatch, wallet debit, courier assignment or payout release happens from the review sheet.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added a dark `package-review-sheet` with mobile-first route review styling, Reference OS overrides, quote metrics, safety checks, provider-led payment boundary and compact action buttons.
  - added draft/review helpers for package estimates, review metrics, safety checks, billing copy and a modern quote-ready screen.
  - changed `App.confirmPackageDelivery()` to open `Review package route` instead of creating the order immediately.
  - added `App.finalizePackageDelivery()`, `App.editPackageDeliveryDraft()` and `App.cancelPackageDeliveryDraft()` so route creation/client billing stays behind explicit review confirmation.
  - changed the quote success surface to use the same review visual language and to state that quotes do not charge buyers, assign couriers or settle payouts.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\tools\quality-loop-artbook.mjs`:
  - strengthened package delivery regressions so standalone and business package flows must show `[data-package-review]`, at least four metrics, four safety checks, provider-led boundary copy and no horizontal overflow.

## Home Player Pay Lens Collapse Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-06-02 17:22 +09:30` from the automated founder/product QA loop, continuing the floating payment/AI affordance polish after the Menu Pay Lens safe dock pass.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, latest QA progress, and checked the latest Pay Lens/Menu proof before editing.
- Highest-value launch-readiness move:
  - collapsed Pay Lens into a compact icon control when the mini-player is active, so the payment helper stays reachable without spanning over home/player content.
  - added stable page/player classes to the outer `#app` host because browser-normalized fixed controls are direct children of the host, not always descendants of the inner `.app` shell.
  - kept the Android app Play Store-safe: Pay Lens still only prepares review drafts and cannot move money.
- Updated `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - added `app-host`, `page-*`, `page-more`, and `has-player` classes to the outer `#app` host during render.

## Android Phone Proof Next Steps Pass
- Follow-up at `2026-06-02 19:11 +09:30` from the automated founder/product QA loop, after the AI UI simplification passes made the remaining launch blocker clearer.
- Re-read `ARTBOOK_PROMPT_MEMORY.md`, `ARTBOOK_POST_WORK_CHECKLIST.md`, the latest QA progress note, and checked ADB before finalizing.
- Highest-value launch-readiness move:
  - made the Android release handoff explicitly action the persistent ADB/offline and foreground-proof blocker.

## Event Closeout Provider Replay Proof Notes V1 Pass
- Time: 2026-06-05 06:26 +09:30.
- Founder/product call: the highest-value launch-readiness move was to give Review Ops a concrete place to attach hosted/backend provider callback replay proof notes without letting the Android APK clear provider replay or move money. This pass keeps provider callback replay backend-owned while making public HTTPS callback, auth, raw-body signature, replay-store, mutation-guard and proof-ledger evidence visible in the event closeout packet.
- Changed `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\src\artbook-mobile.html`:
  - Added event-scoped provider replay proof note discovery and six proof lanes: hosted HTTPS callback URL, Review Ops callback auth, raw-body signature, replay/idempotency store, callback mutation guard and proof-note ledger.
  - Added a visible `Provider replay proof` panel inside the event closeout launch packet, placed after the server idempotency fixture and before organizer KYC/KYB proof.
  - Added provider replay proof note intake and copyable replay proof packet text.
  - Added `App.saveEventCloseoutProviderReplayProofNote` and `App.copyEventCloseoutProviderReplayProofPacket`.
  - Added provider replay proof note counts and lane readiness to the launch packet text and Review Ops next-action evidence.
  - Preserved the boundary: proof notes are Review Ops evidence only. Android still cannot mark provider success, call providers, create receipt candidates, release escrow/refunds/payouts, credit wallets, create spendable balance or recognize founder revenue.

### 2026-06-05 11:16 +09:30 - Backend launch pack: Supabase, Convex, Base44 connector pass
- Scope:
  - Used the connected backend tools from the founder request: Supabase, Convex and Base44.
  - Supabase connector showed organization `montouch's Org` but `projects: []`, so no live Supabase database was available for migration apply.
  - Base44 connector showed no existing Artbook app, so no Base44 builder data model was edited or forked.
  - Convex scaling guidance was used as architecture input: tenant/account IDs on tenant-owned tables, server-side authorization, indexed/paginated hot paths and no client-side filtering for protected data.
  - Added a concrete Supabase launch backend pack under `incoming\Artbook-transfer-v181\supabase\`.
- Changed:
  - `supabase\migrations\20260605014500_artbook_launch_core.sql`
    - 15 launch tables: accounts, memberships, profiles, posts, listings, bookings, booking events, orders, messages, provider events, trust evidence, support cases, AI tasks, outbox and audit events.
    - Forced RLS on every public table and 42 policies using `auth.uid()` plus account memberships.
    - Private helper functions live in `artbook_private`, not the exposed public schema.
    - Provider event writes, outbox writes and audit writes are blocked for authenticated clients.
    - Android creator monetization is blocked at the listing schema level.
  - `supabase\functions\provider-webhook\index.ts`
    - fail-closed Supabase Edge Function for provider callback replay.
    - reads raw body, verifies `ARTBOOK_PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, stores only payload digest/safe shape metadata and never stores raw provider payloads.
    - returns non-settling statuses with `moneyMovementEnabled:false`, provider fetch required, owner approval required and proof before release required.
  - `supabase\config.toml`
    - disables JWT verification only for provider webhooks because external providers do not send user JWTs; the Artbook webhook secret remains required.
  - `tools\supabase-launch-backend-audit.mjs`
    - verifies the Supabase pack safety boundaries: RLS, table count, policy count, no raw payload storage, no client provider-event writes, no money movement and no Android creator monetization.
  - `server\src\server.mjs`
    - fixed the release evidence packet progress reader so it searches known workspace/GitHub QA paths and prefers the newest backend-grade audit section (`backend-smoke-test` or `quality-loop-artbook`) instead of losing backend evidence when the newest QA note is UI-only.
    - added a GitHub-safe APK evidence fallback: local APK file, desktop APK copy, then latest QA-log APK SHA/version/signing evidence, so backend smoke tests do not require forbidden APK binaries in the repo.
  - `server\README.md`, `docs\API_CONTRACT.md`, `docs\BACKEND_HANDOFF.md`, `docs\SUPABASE_LAUNCH_BACKEND.md`
    - documented the launch pack, connector status, apply steps and live blockers.
- Verification:
  - Used bundled Codex Node runtime because the system `node` shim returned Windows `Access is denied`.
  - `tools\supabase-launch-backend-audit.mjs`: passed, 15 tables, 42 RLS policies, raw provider payload storage false, money movement false, Android creator monetization false and client provider-event writes false.
  - `server\src\server.mjs --check`: passed and listed the current endpoint schema.
  - `tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs`: passed 344 checks after the release evidence packet progress-reader fix.
  - GitHub checkout verification under `C:\Users\brown\Documents\GitHub\Artbook\current-mobile`: `tools\supabase-launch-backend-audit.mjs` passed and `tools\backend-smoke-test.mjs` passed 344 checks after the QA-log APK evidence fallback.
- Rebuild / APK:
  - no APK rebuild was needed because this pass changed backend/docs/test artifacts only and did not change the Android HTML source.
- Moto World:
  - no Moto World item was archived because this was a founder-selected backend connector pass, not a Moto World-supplied issue.
  - Moto World remains AI-labeled, owner-controlled and alive.
- Blockers / notes:
  - live Supabase migration apply is blocked until a Supabase project exists in the connected account or a project ref is linked through CI/CLI.
  - local Supabase CLI is not installed.
  - production provider activation still needs server-side secrets, signed raw-body callback proof, provider fetch clients, legal/payment partner review, compliance/tax review and Play release signing.
- Next focus:
  - create or connect the real Supabase project, apply this migration on a development branch, set `ARTBOOK_PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, deploy `provider-webhook`, then run sandbox M-Pesa/card/payout callback replay against the Edge Function.
